pattern: Counting ordered face combinations that reach a target is a bounded integer-composition problem solved by accumulating reachable sums one die at a time.
hints: After placing some dice, all that matters is how many ways each partial sum can be reached; the next die just shifts those counts by 1 through k.
Let ways[s] be the number of ways to reach sum s; rolling one more die spreads each ways[s] into ways[s+1..s+k].
Iterate die by die over a counts array, reduce every running total modulo 1000000007, and read off the count at the target.

edge_inputs.description: target below the minimum possible sum is zero
target above the maximum possible sum is zero
minimum target equals number of dice
maximum target equals dice times faces

stress.description: small dice configurations exercised exhaustively against the brute oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 7)
    k = rng.randint(1, 6)
    target = rng.randint(1, n * k + 2)
    return [n, k, target]
stress.note: n and k kept tiny so the exponential oracle stays fast
